name: Information Systems (IS)
description: This is your primary disciplinary home. TPC (task–technology fit), ECM, TAM, continuance intention are all core IS theories. Most of your citations will likely come from here.
journals: Journal of the Association for Information Systems (JAIS)

name: ICT4D
description: As discussed — your broader contextual umbrella. Positions your work in the developing country / Global South discourse.
journals: Electronic Journal of Information Systems in Developing Countries (EJISDC)
Journal of Information Technology for Development

name: Agricultural Economics / Agricultural Science
description: Agritech is inherently about agricultural productivity and decision-making. Journals in this space increasingly publish on digital tool adoption.
journals: World Development (also crosses into development studies)
NJAS — Wageningen Journal of Life Sciences

name: Development Studies / Development Economics
description: Concerned with poverty, smallholder farming, rural livelihoods. Your SSA context plugs directly into this literature.

name: Innovation & Entrepreneurship
description: Agritech is a growing startup ecosystem in SSA. Product discovery connects to innovation diffusion (Rogers' Diffusion of Innovations theory is relevant here).

name: Marketing & Consumer Behavior
description: Continuance intention has strong parallels with customer retention, loyalty, and repeat purchase behavior. Product discovery overlaps with consumer search behavior and decision journeys.
journals: Journal of the Academy of Marketing Science
Journal of Interactive Marketing (relevant for digital products)

name: Human-Computer Interaction (HCI)
description: Concerned with usability, user experience, and interface design. Closely related to why tools fit or don't fit user tasks.
journals: ACM CHI Conference Proceedings — the premier HCI venue (conference, not journal)

name: Sociology / Rural Sociology
description: Social dynamics, community influence, gender dimensions of technology adoption in SSA.
journals: African Sociological Review (relevant for SSA context)

name: Behavioral Science / Psychology
description: Underpins most IS models — TAM, ECM draw heavily on psychology of behavior and motivation.
journals: Organizational Behavior and Human Decision Processes (very relevant — bridges psychology and management)
Computers in Human Behavior (highly relevant — sits between IS and psychology)

name: Public Policy / e-Government
description: If your agritech tools involve government or NGO-driven initiatives, this field becomes relevant.
journals: Government Information Quarterly (GIQ) — most directly relevant
Journal of Public Administration Research and Theory
Transforming Government: People, Process and Policy
